title: A Stable Right-Sided Quaternion Fourier Regime for Multichannel Spectral Analysis
abstract: This paper defines a stable right-sided discrete quaternion Fourier regime for multichannel spectral analysis. Building on a canonical quaternion lane representation of paired complex channels, it treats each lifted lane as a quaternion-valued sequence x[n] in H and adopts a fixed transform axis i. The forward transform is X[k] = sum_{n=0}^{N-1} x[n] exp(-i 2πnk/N), with inverse x[n] = (1/N) sum_{k=0}^{N-1} X[k] exp(+i 2πnk/N). The contribution is not to survey every quaternion Fourier convention, but to make one public, reproducible choice for downstream WaveEngine-style processing. The paper proves exact inversion, right-linearity over the commuting slice subalgebra C_i, nonnegativity of spectral power, and a Parseval identity sum_n |x[n]|^2 = (1/N) sum_k |X[k]|^2. These results justify the fixed-axis right-sided transform as a deterministic spectral seam for quaternion-lifted multichannel data. The paper does not claim universal superiority over other quaternion Fourier conventions, does not address two-sided or higher-dimensional transforms, and does not yet develop the coherence metrics that sit on top of this spectral regime.
notes: Draft spectral-foundation paper defining the fixed-axis right-sided quaternion Fourier seam used after canonical quaternion lifting. This version proves inversion and Parseval-style invariants but intentionally defers cross-lane coherence and orientation metrics to the next paper in the series.
